question: How are the pre-outcome control and signal features constructed?
hypothesis: This supplement defines transformations only and contains no additional hypothesis.

signal.gamma: Black-Scholes from valid stored IV; T>0
signal.empirical: SPY stable sign-map with frozen naive fallback; SPX empirical unavailable until independently calibrated

target.rule: No forward or outcome field may be constructed

controls.surface: Per-expiry ATM IV from nearest-log-moneyness strike and 25-delta skew=put IV-call IV; linear interpolation between expiries bracketing 30D, otherwise nearest within 10 days; eligible DTE 7-60 and IV>0; delta tolerance 0.10
controls.liquidity: Median (ask-bid)/mid over non-crossed non-negative NBBO with mid>0, plus valid quote fraction
controls.price: close returns over trailing 5/20 sessions; absolute-return HAR daily/5/22 using data through t
controls.calendar: weekday, nominal monthly OpEx calendar distance, business month-end
controls.expiry: OI fraction expiring within 7 and 30 calendar days

validation.as_of: Every row contains only session-t or trailing data

pass_fail.feature_gate: Report per-column coverage; no model gate is evaluated during panel construction

exclusions.invalid_iv: zero GEX and excluded from surface
